title: Avena airoides
taxonomy: Avena airoides belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It is a type of wild oat, closely related to other Avena species. The classification of Avena airoides places it within the tribe Triticeae, which includes other wheat and oat species. Within the genus Avena, Avena airoides is a unique species that is often found in arid and semi-arid regions. Avena airoides is also known by its synonym, Avena abyssinica.

intro: Avena airoides, also known as wild oats or Avena, is a perennial grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, which may aid in relieving stress, anxiety, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Avena airoides include avenanthramides, avenalumens, and beta-glucans, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Avena airoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
